items.title: Bulls to return after March massacre? Elara sees limited downside for Nifty after 11% crash amid Iran-US war
items.summary: After an 11% March crash driven by the escalating Iran–US conflict, Elara Securities says the Nifty may have limited downside ahead. Historical data shows conflicts typically cap drawdowns near 10%, and with valuations now 7% below long-term averages, the brokerage expects a potential rebound as risks gradually ease.

items.title: Wall Street rally ahead? Dow futures jump nearly 500 points on Trump’s reported plans to exit Iran war
items.summary: US stock futures surged after reports suggested Donald Trump may seek to end the Iran conflict, easing investor concerns over prolonged geopolitical tensions. Gains in Dow, Nasdaq and S&P futures come amid stable oil prices and falling bond yields, although uncertainty remains due to ongoing risks in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $67,000 despite geopolitical tensions; demand from long-term holders rises
items.summary: Bitcoin held near the $67,000 level amid persistent geopolitical tensions and macro uncertainty, while mixed price action reflected cautious sentiment. Altcoins largely declined, and overall market capitalisation edged lower. Despite some recovery attempts, volatility remains elevated as investors assess inflation risks, liquidity conditions, and global policy signals.

items.title: PropShare Celestia's Rs 244-cr IPO to open on Apr 10
items.summary: Property Share Investment Trust, India's first registered SM REIT, announced its Rs 244.65 crore IPO for PropShare Celestia will open on April 10. The issue, priced between Rs 10-10.50 lakh per unit, aims to acquire seven floors in Ahmedabad's Venus Stratum. This SM REIT's proceeds will fund the acquisition of a fully occupied commercial building with four anchor tenants.

items.title: IndiQube leases 48,000 sq ft office space in Bengaluru to GCC, eyes Rs 75 cr rent in 5 yrs
items.summary: IndiQube Spaces Ltd has secured a significant deal in Bengaluru. The realty firm leased 48,000 sq ft of office space. A health technology firm will establish a global capability centre there. The agreement is valued at Rs 75 crore over five years. This marks a key expansion for IndiQube's managed office solutions.

items.title: Planning to invest in smallcaps? These top 5 funds deliver 28%+ annualised returns in 3 years
items.summary: Top small-cap mutual funds have delivered over 28% annualised returns in three years, attracting investors seeking high-growth opportunities despite higher risk.

items.title: Why The Toer De Geuze Might Be The Ultimate Beer Geek Experience
items.summary: The 2026 edition of Belgium’s biennial Toer De Geuze will open the doors to 14 traditional lambic breweries in a land recognized by UNESCO for their beer culture.

items.title: Why JBS Meat Packing Workers Are On An Historic Strike.
items.summary: JBS meat packing workers in Greeley, Colorado have been on strike since March 16th, the first such strike in 40 years.

items.title: U.S. Must Pass Three Critical Tests To Safeguard National Security
items.summary: The U.S.—and the Free World—faces a grim future if we mishandle how we deal with Iran, Ukraine and Taiwan.

items.summary: Fans of the Philadelphia Phillies didn’t hold back as the New York Mets’ former outfielder powered another win against them.
